touch.x64.o.name: touch
touch.x64.o.quick_description: Create files and modify timestamps (mimics Unix touch). Timestamps are local time.
touch.x64.o.description:
Create empty files or modify all three NTFS timestamps (creation, access, write).
Timestamps are interpreted as local time on the target machine.
The format is auto-detected: numeric strings are parsed as MMDDhhmm, anything else is treated as a freeform date.

Examples:
  touch C:\Windows\Temp\test.txt                       Create file (or update to now)
  touch C:\Windows\Temp\test.txt 01131500               Stamp to Jan 13, 3:00 PM (current year)
  touch C:\Windows\Temp\test.txt 202301011200           Stamp to Jan 1, 2023 12:00 PM
  touch C:\Windows\Temp\test.txt 2501131500.30          Stamp with seconds
  touch C:\Windows\Temp\test.txt "2020-06-15 08:30:00"  Freeform date string
  touch C:\Windows\Temp\test.txt "Jan 13 2025 3pm"      Freeform date string
  touch C:\Windows\Temp\test.txt "" 1                   Update to now, skip if missing
  touch C:\Windows\Temp\test.txt 01131500 1             Stamp only if file exists

Numeric timestamp formats:
  MMDDhhmm              8 chars, current year assumed
  YYMMDDhhmm           10 chars, 20xx century assumed
  CCYYMMDDhhmm         12 chars, full year
  Append .ss for seconds (e.g. 01131500.30)

Freeform dates:
  Any format Windows can parse: "2020-06-15", "Jan 13 2025 3pm", etc.

args.name: nocreate
args.description: 1 to skip file creation if missing (default: 0)

test_bof.x64.o.name: test_bof
test_bof.x64.o.description:
Baseline BOF that prints a success message with the args buffer length.
Used by the test pipeline to verify the BOF loader is working.

Examples:
  test_bof              Prints: BOF executed successfully! args_len=0
